lang.label: Language
lang.help: Spanish is the source language; English and French are complete translations.

common.footer: Systemic Tau Platform · v1.0 · Aligned with systemictau, nested-recd, and CCTP/SDDB

home.tagline: From theory to practice: teach, compute, and falsify Systemic Tau + RECD
home.description: Educational and research platform that unifies foundations, mathematics, domains, a reproducible laboratory, and reference evidence (CCTP/SDDB and more).
home.goals_title: By the end of this home page you should be able to
home.goal_1: Understand which question τ_s answers (relational reorganization) versus classical EWS.
home.goal_2: Distinguish the operational core (τ_s, RECD, surrogates, hash) from Lab extensions (TDA, Breathing Window).
home.goal_3: Choose a domain and a study path (theory → sandbox → Lab → evidence).
home.audience: Audience: researchers, graduate students, and professionals who need rigor, ontological connections, and reproducible experimentation — not a generic time-series dashboard.
home.card_ordinal_body: Observables based on order and conjunctions: the question is not only “how much variance?”, but “how do relationships reorder?”.
home.card_recd_body: Φ₁ · Φ₂ · Φ₃ and excess3 as a grammar of the system’s internal clock (Chronos vs Kairos, in practice).
home.card_falsable_body: Phase-shuffle surrogates, classical EWS panel in parallel, and a SHA-256 hash for every run.
home.achieve_pipe_body: τ_s + RECD (Φ₁–Φ₃, excess3) + classical EWS + surrogates + report with SHA-256 hash.
home.achieve_ev_body: CCTP/SDDB N=10: classical panel often ambiguous; relational panel significant; sign concordance 8/10.
home.achieve_curr_body: Foundations in 6 modules, Bandt–Pompe sandbox, domains, FAQ, glossary, and a 6-week syllabus.
home.achieve_tda_body: Operational in the Lab (Full mode or checkboxes): adaptive W + β₀/β₁ curves. Extensions of the core — they do not replace it.
home.pick_domain_cap: Customize the narrative: the paradigm is the same; the interpretation context changes.
home.active_cardio: Active domain: {domain}. Suggested path: Foundations (τ_s + EWS limits) → Cardiology under Domains → cardio-like Lab or samples 38/51 → CCTP Evidence.
home.active_other: Active domain: {domain}. Start with Foundations (same grammar), read the domain, then try a synthetic series in the Lab and compare with the CCTP anchor under Evidence.
home.route_1_body: Foundations: τ_s, why var/AR1 fail, RECD Φ₁–Φ₃, excess3.
home.route_2_body: Mathematics (BP sandbox) + synthetic or cardio-like Lab with dual reading.
home.route_3_body: CCTP N=10, comparison matrix, scope FAQ and citation.
home.nav_fund_body: Six modules with objectives, analogies, and mini-syntheses.
home.nav_math_body: Interactive Bandt–Pompe and a full-pipeline demo.
home.nav_lab_body: Guided analysis + how to interpret each metric.
home.nav_ev_body: What is already published and the CCTP pilot explained.
home.about_body:
*Systemic Tau Platform* unifies Systemic Tau and RECD in a university-grade educational/scientific product: theory, mathematics, domain applications, interactive laboratory, learning path, and published evidence.

Code aligned with `systemictau`, `nested-recd`, and the **CCTP/SDDB** pilot.

**Brand principle:** it is not a standalone notebook; it is an environment where one can *define, compute, compare, test against a null model, and document* the paradigm.

**CLI:** `stp analyze data.csv --domain cardiology -o report.md` · `stp serve`
home.start_cap: Three doors. Everything else lives under Explore.
home.start_research_body: Lab: data → parameters → run → dual reading.
home.start_teach_body: Syllabus, downloadable handouts, and classroom packs.
home.explore_cap: Foundations, metrics, domains, and shortcuts — collapsed so the entry stays calm.

fundamentos.title: Theoretical foundations
fundamentos.subtitle: Precise definitions, limits of classical EWS, RECD ontology, and the platform’s scientific stance: dual panel and falsifiability.
fundamentos.goal_1: State the τ_s question versus var/AR1.
fundamentos.goal_2: Describe Φ₁, Φ₂, Φ₃ and why excess3 is primary under noisy data.
fundamentos.goal_3: Apply dual reading CSD ↔ relational reorganization.
fundamentos.sidebar_path:
1. Systemic Tau  
2. EWS limits  
3. Nested RECD  
4. excess3 (primary under noise)  
5. CSD — alignment and divergence  
6. Operational philosophy
fundamentos.micro_blurb: One control → one figure → one reading. It does not replace the full Laboratory.
fundamentos.micro_tau_coupling_help: After the midpoint of the series, the two variables couple with this strength.
fundamentos.micro_tau_out: Mean τ_s pre={pre:.4f} · post={post:.4f} · Δ≈{d:+.4f} (coupling={c:.2f}). Observe whether Δ grows with coupling.
fundamentos.micro_ews_prompt: Compare classical signals (var, AR1), standardized, against τ_s on the same coupling transition.
fundamentos.micro_ews_note: If var/AR1 are ambiguous but τ_s moves (or the reverse), note the disagreement: that is the core of dual reading.
fundamentos.micro_recd_cap: Φ₁ local · Φ₂ relational · Φ₃ global. Check whether any level reorganizes after the switch (mid-series).
fundamentos.micro_ex_out: mean excess3={mean:.4f} · Δ(post−pre)={d:+.4f} · θ₃={th:.2f}. Raise θ₃: the excess becomes stricter.
fundamentos.micro_csd_prompt: Series with rising volatility (critical-slowing style). Does variance always rise together with τ_s?
fundamentos.micro_csd_note: Classical CSD watches var/AR1; τ_s watches joint ordinal reorganization. They may diverge — document when they do.
fundamentos.micro_phil_prompt: Minimal metrics for an honest claim: magnitude, symbolic patterns, and (if applicable) an event.
fundamentos.micro_phil_event: Assume an event at the midpoint
fundamentos.micro_phil_event_on: Design event at t≈{t0}. Without an event, Δ is still computed by halves — declare the design.
fundamentos.micro_phil_event_off: No declared event: any Δ is exploratory.
fundamentos.micro_phil_caution: Not a clinical diagnosis or a forecast. It is a pedagogical experiment; the full Lab adds a reproducibility hash.
fundamentos.domain_lens_help: Pick a field (e.g. Cardiology). Each tab shows, in plain language, what τ_s / RECD would mean there. The math does not change — the vocabulary and scientific question do. Not a medical diagnosis or automatic forecast.
fundamentos.domain_lens_select: Which domain should the examples speak?

matematica.title: Mathematics and sandbox
matematica.subtitle: Bandt–Pompe, ordinal pipeline, and operational extensions (Breathing, TDA, memory).
matematica.goal_1: Build Bandt–Pompe symbols and observe their monotonic invariance.
matematica.goal_2: Run a mini τ_s + RECD pipeline on a short series.
matematica.goal_3: Place Breathing / TDA / memory as extensions, not as the core.
matematica.extensions: Operational extensions: Breathing, TDA, ordinal memory
matematica.ext_table:
| Extension | Idea | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **Breathing window** | Higher local volatility → shorter W when estimating τ_s | Lab · checkbox / Extensions tab |
| **TDA / Betti** | β₀/β₁ curves of the state cloud in windows | Lab · Full or TDA checkbox |
| **Ordinal memory** | Lag-1 symbolic MI and cross-MI | Lab · Memory checkbox |
matematica.ext_note: The Lab **does not depend** on these extensions. The main claim remains τ_s + RECD + EWS + surrogates.
matematica.sandbox_callout: Change m and delay: the alphabet has size m!. Monotone transforms of the series do not change the symbols.
matematica.demo_body:
**Coupled logistic maps** with a mid-series regime switch.
This is the “toy laboratory”: you *know* the switch time (Chronos)
and can check whether the relational panel reacts.
matematica.mode_help: Fast: few surrogates. Full: richer exploration.
matematica.ext_body:
These modules extend the core; they do not replace τ_s + RECD + EWS + surrogates.

{table}

dominios.title: Application domains
dominios.subtitle: The method is the same everywhere. What changes is the context—and how much real evidence backs each area.
dominios.goal_1: Tell teaching demos apart from the domain with documented real evidence.
dominios.goal_2: Pick a domain, read its card, and open the Lab with the right example.
dominios.goal_3: Do not claim clinical, outbreak, or trading prediction without external validation.
dominios.select: Choose the domain you want to study
dominios.open_lab_domain: Open Lab with this domain’s demo →
dominios.anchor_callout: In one sentence: only Cardiology has a documented real pilot on the platform (CCTP / SDDB Holter database). The other domains are practice examples—synthetic series or designed demos to teach the method. Use them to learn and transfer the τ_s + RECD grammar, not to claim the method is already validated for dengue, markets, climate, etc.
dominios.synth_header: Before a domain: Lab synthetic controls
dominios.synth_callout: The Lab also has two calibrators that are not “real-world domains”: (1) coupled logistic maps—you should see a clear coupling change; (2) AR noise—relational signal should be near absent. Practise there before interpreting cardiology or dengue-like demos.
dominios.ref_callout: Lab example: {data}. Evidence level in v1.0: {maturity}. After reading the card, open the Laboratory with this domain’s preset.
dominios.open_body: This loads the **{name}** example with domain default parameters (and a marked event when the dataset provides one).
dominios.voice_header: How to talk about results in this domain
dominios.map_intro: This is not a ranking of importance. It is a scientific-honesty guide: it tells you how strongly you may speak about each area in a report or thesis.
dominios.legend_body:
| Label | What it means for you |
|---|---|
| **Very high** | A real cohort/pilot is cited on the platform. Still research—not a clinical device. |
| **High / Medium–high** | Mature narrative and demos; limited or external real evidence. Use *methodological transfer* claims. |
| **Medium** | Good classroom example. Designed ground truth (the “event” is built for teaching). |
| **Low–medium** | Toy model or sandbox. Pipeline practice only; not field evidence. |

**Card data line** (e.g. “SDDB / CCTP” or “Dengue-like demo”) = where the Lab series come from, not a clinical quality seal.

lab.title: Interactive laboratory
lab.subtitle: Full pipeline: catalog or CSV → roles and event → domain preset → τ_s + RECD + EWS + surrogates → dual reading → export (MD / JSON / Methods).
lab.goal_1: Load a dataset with ground truth or a marked event and apply the domain preset.
lab.goal_2: Interpret Δτ_s, excess3, and p_surr with dual reading bounded to the study design.
lab.goal_3: Export report, Methods, and JSON with a reproducibility hash.
lab.data_blurb: **Object of study.** Catalog with ground truth · domain generators · your own CSV.
lab.vars_help: N≥2 ideal; 1 column → proxy z(x), z(|Δx|).
lab.empty_csv: Drop a CSV or load a dataset from the catalog.
lab.shape_cap: Shape: {n} × {v} · domain `{domain}` · {ev}. If N=1, the pipeline builds the bivariate proxy automatically.
lab.mode_help: Fast: exploration (~a few seconds). Full: more surrogates + ordinal memory.
lab.null_help: Phase-shuffle: spectrum. IAAFT: spectrum + amplitude distribution.
lab.breathing_help: Adaptive W: higher local volatility → shorter window (operational in the Lab).
lab.tda_help: β₀/β₁ curves in windows (ripser if installed; otherwise VR 1-skeleton). Extension: does not replace τ_s.
lab.need_data: Load data from the catalog or a CSV to continue.
lab.ext_blurb: **Operational extensions of the core** — useful for pedagogical contrast; the main claim remains τ_s + RECD + EWS + surrogates.
lab.ext_off: Enable **Breathing window** and/or **TDA / Betti** under parameters and run again. In Full mode they are proposed on by default.
lab.empty_run: Configure parameters and press **Run full analysis**.
lab.ui_mode_help: Novice hides extensions and uses catalog presets. Expert exposes all controls.
lab.ui_novice_cap: Guided mode: one step at a time, domain presets, no TDA/breathing.
lab.ui_expert_cap: Full control: window, m, surrogates, breathing, TDA, and ordinal memory.
lab.catalog_defaults: Catalog defaults: W={window}, stride={stride}, m={m}, θ₃={theta3}
lab.novice_params_note: In **First analysis** mode we use the dataset/domain preset. Switch to **Expert** to edit window, m, and extensions.
lab.novice_summary: Will run with **W={window}**, stride={stride}, m={m}, θ₃={theta3}, n_surr={n_surr}, null `{surr}`.
lab.run_summary: Domain **{domain}** · W={window} · stride={stride} · m={m} · mode `{mode}` · n_surr={n_surr} · null `{surr}`
lab.how_to_read_body:
- **Δτ_s**: mean change in Systemic Tau (post − pre half, or relative to the event). Large magnitude suggests ordinal reorganization.
- **excess3 / Δexcess3**: excess coincidence at RECD level Φ₃; increases may indicate more rigid structural alignment.
- **p_surr (τ_s)**: p-value of the surrogate null on Δτ_s. Small p + non-trivial |Δτ_s| → the change is hard to explain by the null spectrum/noise alone.
- **Dual reading** compares τ_s/RECD with classical EWS (var, AR1): they need not agree — that is pedagogical.
lab.dl_zip_help: Series + params + result JSON + report.md + Methods + REPRO_HASH — for hand-in or resuming work.
lab.domain_voice_panel: How Tau sounds in this domain
lab.how_to_read_domain: In **{domain}**, read Δτ_s and excess3 as reorganization of **{variables}** relative to **{event}**. {coach}

ruta.title: Learning path
ruta.subtitle: In-depth FAQ, glossary, and paths by role (researcher, student, instructor).
ruta.goal_1: Find answers to frequent graduate-level and peer-review misunderstandings.
ruta.goal_2: Use the glossary as a terminological anchor.
ruta.goal_3: Choose a short path to the Lab with a concrete dataset.
ruta.subtitle_full: Progression Basic → Intermediate → Advanced, glossary, FAQ, and core scope.
ruta.goal_1_full: Place yourself at a level (Basic / Intermediate / Advanced) with a session checklist.
ruta.goal_2_full: Use the glossary to disambiguate terms before the Lab.
ruta.goal_3_full: Know the core scope (what is computed and how to cite with rigor).
ruta.path_callout: Check each item as you complete it. Progress is stored in the browser session.
ruta.lvl1_body: Ordinal vocabulary, why var/AR1 are not enough, first synthetic Lab.
ruta.lvl2_body: RECD, excess3, CCTP, surrogates, and dual reading in the Lab.
ruta.lvl3_body: Study design, TDA/BW horizons, primary literature, and citation.
ruta.b1: Read Foundations → Systemic Tau (objectives + orchestra analogy)
ruta.b2: Read Foundations → EWS limits (five failures + dual reading)
ruta.b3: Glossary: τ_s, RECD, Bandt–Pompe, Kairos, dual reading
ruta.b5: Lab: synthetic coupled logistic maps, Fast mode, note Δτ_s and p_surr
ruta.i1: Foundations: RECD Φ₁–Φ₃ + excess3 (why the continuous proxy?)
ruta.i2: Cardiology CCTP domain + samples 38/51 or cardio-like
ruta.i3: Lab: compare RECD tab vs classical EWS out loud
ruta.i4: Surrogates: interpret p without forgetting effect size
ruta.i5: Dengue domain (transfer of the same grammar)
ruta.i6: Export MD report + hash in the notebook / assignment
ruta.a1: Read Core scope (interpretation limits + what to cite)
ruta.a2: Breathing and TDA as operational Lab extensions (not the main claim)
ruta.a3: Design a mini-study with a reproducibility hash
ruta.a4: Read Magna Synthesis / CCTP preprint and contrast with the Lab
ruta.a5: Write a methods paragraph a reviewer cannot misread
ruta.exp_blurb: Each milestone ends with an analysis, not reading alone.
ruta.gloss_blurb: Define the term *before* using it in an analysis. Long entries flag interpretation traps.
ruta.checklist_saved: Checklist synced to your account (when signed in).

evidencia.title: Evidence and CCTP anchor
evidencia.subtitle: What is published, what the SDDB pilot shows, and how to cite without overclaiming.
evidencia.goal_1: Summarize the CCTP/SDDB N=10 result in dual-panel language.
evidencia.goal_2: Distinguish anchor evidence from pedagogical demos.
evidencia.goal_3: Open the Lab with SDDB samples when available.
evidencia.subtitle_full: CCTP/SDDB anchor, regenerable figures, and publications linked to the paradigm.
evidencia.goal_1_full: State the CCTP pilot finding without overclaiming.
evidencia.goal_2_full: Regenerate dual-reading figures from the demo sample.
evidencia.goal_3_full: Locate primary publications and what may be cited.
evidencia.anchor_callout: In the N=10 pilot, the classical panel is often ambiguous while the relational panel (τ_s / RECD) is more informative; sign concordance 8/10 under the primary null (phase-shuffle).
evidencia.fig_callout: The figure is computed in the session from the demo sample (not a static image). Parameters are simplified for UI speed.
evidencia.open_same: Open the same series in the Lab →
evidencia.press_regen: Press the button to generate the session figure.
evidencia.cmp_body: This matrix does not say “we are better at everything”: it says **which scientific question** each family covers. Tau+RECD stands out in ordinal interpretability, multivariate structure, and a *clock model* (RECD), with a strong surrogate culture.
evidencia.scope_table:
| Claim supported in v1.0 | Out of scope |
|-------------------------|--------------|
| Measurable relational reorganization pre-VF in the pilot cohort | “Predicts sudden death in the ICU” |
| Classical panel can be ambiguous while the relational one is not | “Classical EWS are refuted” |
| excess3 is more robust than binary Φ₃ on noisy RR | “Universal θ₃ for all domains” |
| The Lab reproduces the analysis grammar (pipeline + nulls + hash) | “The Lab hash validates a clinical trial” |

biblioteca.title: Research library
biblioteca.subtitle: Classified catalog of the full Publications corpus: papers, preprints, syntheses, thesis and notes — with download when the file is on this machine.
biblioteca.goal_1: Find a work by collection, domain, year or keyword.
biblioteca.goal_2: See at a glance which question each text answers (plain-language summary).
biblioteca.goal_3: Download the local PDF/DOCX or open the DOI / Preprints.org link when available.
biblioteca.root_ok: Local corpus connected: {path} · {available} of {total} records have a file on disk.
biblioteca.root_missing: Publications folder not found on this machine.
biblioteca.root_help: Set environment variable STP_PUBLICATIONS_DIR to your PDF/DOCX folder, or edit root_path in content/evidencia/research_library.yaml. Metadata remains available; only binary download will fail.
biblioteca.no_featured: No featured items in the catalog.
biblioteca.no_match: No records match the filters. Broaden the search.
biblioteca.file_offline: File not available on this machine

docencia.title: Teaching (6 weeks)
docencia.subtitle: Syllabus, rubric, and materials ready for a graduate block or intensive workshop.
docencia.goal_1: Follow the 6-week syllabus with clear deliverables.
docencia.goal_2: Use the dual-reading and claim-ethics rubric.
docencia.subtitle_full: Professional 6-week syllabus (competencies, 0–2 rubric, datasets, ethics) aligned with STP v1.0 core.
docencia.goal_1_full: Build a short course with C1–C7 competencies, weekly Lab, and CCTP anchor.
docencia.goal_2_full: Assess with a 12-point rubric (dual reading, nulls, scoped claims, hash).
docencia.goal_3_full: Distribute handouts and instructor/student packs in ES/EN/FR without a payments backend.
docencia.comp_callout: Integrative competency: define, compute, compare, null-test, and document a τ_s + RECD analysis with dual reading, surrogates, and reproducible Methods, scoping claims to domain maturity (CCTP anchor; methodological transfer elsewhere). Not clinical-device, operational-alert, or trading training.
docencia.syl_table:
WkTopicLab / deliverableApp anchor
1Classical EWS, CSD, univariate limitsvar/AR1 on synthetic · 1-page essay · ethics of scopeFoundations EWS/CSD · Ethics
2Bandt–Pompe & τ_s · controlscoupled_logistic vs ar_noise · hash · n_surr≥8Mathematics · Lab
3RECD, excess3 & dual readingRECD panel · 1–2 pp. mini-report (question + scope)Foundations 3–4 · Dual reading
4CCTP / pre-VF anchorcardiac_like or SDDB 38/51 · dual report 2–3 pp. (30%)Cardio domains · Evidence
5Transfer (1 domain)Epi/neuro/eco/climate/edu/sleep/social/fin · mini-report (25%)Domains · presets
6Surrogates, ethics, portfolioMD+JSON+Methods export · checklist · peer review (45%)Lab · Materials

Load: 4–5 h/wk · 24–30 h total · practical pass ≥9/12 · download full syllabus (v1.1) below.
docencia.rubric_body:
| # | Criterion | 2 (max) |
|---|-----------|---------|
| 1 | Scientific question | Relational + justified cut/event |
| 2 | Parameters & preset | Preset + W/stride/m/θ₃/n_surr/seed documented |
| 3 | Core metrics | Δτ_s, mean/Δ excess3, p_surr (or n_surr=0 declared) |
| 4 | Parallel EWS | Dual reading: confirms / silent / contradicts |
| 5 | Scoped conclusion | Claims match domain maturity + non-scope sentence |
| 6 | Reproducibility | Hash + Methods + peer-usable export |

**Practical pass:** ≥ **9/12**. **Excellent:** ≥ 11/12.  
Full 0/1/2 levels and portfolio weights: handout *6-week syllabus* v1.1.
docencia.card_md_b: Foundations with objectives, domains, FAQ, glossary, and core scope in `content/`.
docencia.card_lab_b: Generators, presets, surrogates, and report — without waiting for full TDA.
docencia.card_code_b: `systemictau` · `nested-recd` · CCTP — same grammar as the papers.
docencia.lic_table:
UseCondition
AcademicFree with citation of the software and domain papers
CommercialProfessional or Institutional license (contact authors)
Third-party dataSubject to PhysioNet, LTER, DengAI, etc. terms of use
docencia.nb_note: Until P2 notebooks exist, the Lab + content/ + syllabus v1.1 fully support the 6-week course. Do not delay the course waiting for notebooks.

materiales.title: Downloadable materials
materiales.subtitle: Guides, handouts, FAQ, glossary, and packs in Markdown ready to print or share.
materiales.goal_1: Download the student or instructor pack for the course.
materiales.goal_2: Use the Lab cheatsheet in the first practical session.
materiales.goal_3: Cite scope and ethics together with the Lab report.
materiales.all_note: All texts are generated from the Spanish source and their localized translations.
materiales.format_callout: Format: all files are .md (Markdown). You can convert to PDF with Pandoc (pandoc file.md -o file.pdf) or paste them into your LMS. These are pedagogical materials, not a commercial product manual.
materiales.filter_help: Leave empty to see all materials.
materiales.howto_body:
1. **LMS / Classroom:** upload the `.md` or convert it to PDF.  
2. **Pandoc → PDF:** `pandoc stp_pack_estudiante.md -o pack.pdf --toc`  
3. **Quick print:** open the Markdown in VS Code / Typora and export.  
4. **Course git:** version the packs alongside the syllabus.  

The **student pack** usually suffices for a short course.  
The **instructor pack** includes syllabus, manual, and glossary.
materiales.pdf_unavailable: PDF unavailable in this environment (requires Pandoc + LaTeX). Use Markdown or install the typesetting toolchain.
materiales.pdf_ok: Pandoc PDF available for packs and handouts.
materiales.pdf_failed: Could not generate PDF (Pandoc + LaTeX required?).

interp.title: Dual reading (automatic, qualified)
interp.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} of ordinal coupling across segments.
interp.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): irreducible ordinal synergy {trend} in the segment.
interp.surr_reject: p_surr(τ_s) = {p:.3f}: the observed Δ is extreme versus the null (phase-shuffle/IAAFT) → candidate residual relational structure.
interp.surr_marginal: p_surr(τ_s) = {p:.3f}: marginal; report effect size and design, not p alone.
interp.surr_null: p_surr(τ_s) = {p:.3f}: the cross-independence null is not rejected for this Δ; do not interpret as strong relational reorganization.
interp.surr_off: Surrogates not computed (n=0): no p_surr to qualify Δ.
interp.event_on: Event marked at index t = {t}: pre/post Δ use that cut (not a simple half/half split).
interp.event_off: No event index: Δ uses a temporal split first half vs second half of the metric series (exploratory design).
interp.tda_bullet: TDA (extension, backend={backend}): Δβ₀={db0:+.3f}, Δβ₁={db1:+.3f} — topology of the state cloud in windows; complements, does not replace, τ_s/excess3.
interp.breathing_bullet: Breathing window active: W contracted/expanded with local volatility (adaptive-resolution τ_s; document W_min–W_max in Methods).
interp.summary_concord: Dual reading: τ_s and excess3 move in the same direction (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}.
interp.summary_quiet: Dual reading: weak ordinal reorganization in this design (small Δ).
interp.summary_mixed: Mixed dual reading: τ_s and excess3 do not agree in sign/magnitude (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Prioritize context and classical EWS.
interp.caution_tail: Always compare with var/AR1: the classical panel may confirm, stay silent, or contradict. TDA and breathing are extensions of the core; do not base the main claim on them alone.
interp.dom_cardiology: Cardio domain: not a diagnosis or clinical device; methodological proxy / research cohort.
interp.dom_epidemiology: Epi domain: interpret with seasonality and surveillance quality.
interp.dom_neuroscience: Neuro domain: preprocessing and artifacts dominate; validate channels.
interp.dom_ecology: Ecology domain: long timescales; few points → short windows.
interp.dom_finance: Finance domain: not a trading signal; illustrative volatility regimes.
interp.dom_climate: Climate domain: pedagogical regime demo; not operational forecast.
interp.dom_social: Social domain: toy model; not social prediction.
interp.dom_education: Learning domain: cohort sandbox; not production LMS analytics.
interp.dom_physiology: Sleep domain: physiological bridge; not clinical scoring.
interp.dom_synthetic: Synthetic series with ground truth (switch/event): ideal to calibrate dual reading.
interp.dom_generic: Qualify the conclusion by the domain’s empirical maturity.

etica.title: Ethics and core scope
etica.description: What is ready, what is not, honest claims, and citation.

extensiones.title: Extensions — Breathing and TDA/Betti
extensiones.description: How to enable and read adaptive W and β₀/β₁ curves (operational in the Lab).

faq.title: FAQ (deep answers)
faq.description: Graduate-level misunderstandings, data, ethics, and pedagogical domains.

glosario.title: Glossary
glosario.description: Terms τ_s, RECD, excess3, EWS, surrogates… generated from glossary.yaml.

pack_estudiante.title: Student pack (single MD)
pack_estudiante.description: Quick guide + theory + math + cheatsheet + dual reading + checklist + FAQ + ethics.

pack_docente.title: Teacher pack (single MD)
pack_docente.description: Syllabus + ethics + manual + theory/lab packs + FAQ + glossary.

fundamentos_compilado.title: Compiled foundations
fundamentos_compilado.description: Concatenates content/fundamentos/ modules into one Markdown.

handouts.pack_student_sub: Essential materials to take the Lab and submit with dual reading.
handouts.pack_teacher_sub: Syllabus, ethics, operations, and all classroom handouts in a single file.

domain_hints.cardiology: Cardio CCTP: W≈101, RR+|ΔRR| proxy. Ask about pre-VF reorganization, not a “clinical alarm”.
domain_hints.epidemiology: Epi: short windows (weeks). cases–climate / surveillance; seasonality before claiming a regime.
domain_hints.neuroscience: Neuro/EEG: artifacts and channels dominate. τ_s = ordinal coupling across sensors, not a spike detector.
domain_hints.ecology: Lakes / bloom: few observations → moderate W. Bridge to ecological CSD, not eutrophication forecast.
domain_hints.finance: Illustrative volatility regimes. τ_s is not a trading signal or alpha.
domain_hints.climate: Temp–precip–soil under latent drought. Regime demo; not operational forecast.
domain_hints.social: Toy polarization with designed switch. Collective grammar, not social prediction.
domain_hints.education: Synthetic learning cohort. Pedagogical sandbox, not production LMS analytics.
domain_hints.physiology: Toy sleep / HRV. Physiological bridge; not clinical AASM scoring.
domain_hints.synthetic: Ground truth (switch): calibrate dual reading before real data.

plots.ews_title: Normalized comparison: τ_s vs classical EWS
plots.breathing_off: Breathing window not computed — enable the checkbox and re-run.
plots.tda_off: TDA not computed — enable the TDA/Betti checkbox and re-run.

domain_blurbs.cardiology: Only domain with a real pilot in the app (pre-VF, SDDB Holter). Use this as the reference for a serious report.
domain_blurbs.epidemiology: Outbreak demo (cases + climate). Practise method transfer; not operational disease surveillance.
domain_blurbs.neuroscience: Multichannel EEG-like coupling change. Practice grammar; not a clinical seizure predictor.
domain_blurbs.ecology: Lake demo (nutrients / bloom). Bridge to classical ecosystem early-warning literature.
domain_blurbs.climate: Drought-regime demo (temp–rain–soil). Dual-reading practice; not a climate alert system.
domain_blurbs.education: Classroom/cohort demo (engagement, peers, load). Meta-teaching example; not a real LMS analysis.
domain_blurbs.physiology: Sleep demo (HRV, activity, temperature). Physiological bridge to cardio; not medical sleep scoring.
domain_blurbs.social: Simple opinion-polarization toy. Method only; does not predict real politics.
domain_blurbs.finance: Volatility-regime demo. Method transfer only; not investment advice or trading signals.

illus.tau_cap: Left: amplitude grows (var↑) but the relation graph does not change. Right: similar amplitude; edges reorder — that is what τₛ measures.
illus.dual_cap: Two instruments on the same stretch. The classical panel may diverge (var↑, AR1↓); the relational panel is read in parallel without picking a side.
illus.recd_cap: Φ₁–Φ₃ nest: coincidence, persistence, and synergy. The clock advances with reorganization weight (Kairos), not only with the CSV index (Chronos).
illus.bp_cap: With m=3 there are 3! = 6 order patterns. The series becomes a permutation alphabet; cross-channel coincidence feeds Φ₁.
illus.surr_cap: Phase-shuffle preserves each channel’s spectrum and destroys cross-dependence. If Δτₛ / excess3 beat the null, the signal is not reducible to independent auto-spectra.
illus.pipe_cap: Data → ordinal → τ_s / RECD → EWS → surrogates → dual interpretation and hashed export.
illus.ck_cap: Chronos is the external clock (index). Kairos accumulates ticks weighted by system reorganization.
illus.cctp_cap: Pre-VF: classical panel often ambiguous; relational (τ_s, excess3) with phase-shuffle nulls anchors v1.0.
illus.ex_title: Why excess3 is primary under noise
illus.ex_cap: Binary Φ₃ can stay zero on noisy RR; excess3 keeps the continuous irreducible-synergy signal.
illus.hero_cap: From the relational question to the executable Lab: τ_s, RECD, EWS, nulls, and dual reading.
illus.svg_phi1_q: Do channels share the same ordinal pattern?
illus.svg_bp_cards: Each card is a local order pattern (low ranks → short bars).
illus.svg_nodes: Highlighted nodes: relational core and reproducibility seal
illus.svg_hero_foot: One core · five domains of interpretation

cardiology.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ ordinal coupling between RR level and beat-to-beat variation — not SDNN or RMSSD.
cardiology.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ irreducible ordinal synergy in the pre-VF approach; often more robust than binary Φ₃ on noisy RR.
cardiology.classic_gloss: RR var/AR1: in CCTP often var↑ and AR1↓ (classical panel ambiguous vs relational reorganization).
cardiology.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal RR–|ΔRR| coupling across segments (CCTP proxy).
cardiology.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): irreducible pre-event ordinal synergy {trend}.
cardiology.event_on: Cut at VF onset / Holter event t = {t}: pre/approach vs post Δ use that clinical series marker.
cardiology.event_off: No VF marker: Δ uses first vs second half (exploratory; in CCTP ideally set vfon).
cardiology.summary_concord: Cardio dual reading: RR–variability relational reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. CCTP methodological proxy — not a clinical alarm.
cardiology.summary_quiet: Cardio dual reading: weak ordinal reorganization. Check pacing, artifacts, interpolation, and W≈101.
cardiology.summary_mixed: Mixed cardio dual reading: τ_s and excess3 disagree (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Cross-check var/AR1 and Holter quality.
cardiology.lab_coach: Guiding question for students: does the relationship between heart-rate level and beat-to-beat variability reorganize before the marker, even when classical measures (variance, AR1) are confusing?
cardiology.example_tau:
In computational cardiology research, τ_s does not mean “this patient will get sick” or “will die”.

It measures whether the relationship between (1) the time between beats (RR, in milliseconds) and (2) how much that interval changes from beat to beat (|ΔRR|) reorganizes in the segment before an event of interest.

In the CCTP pilot that event is ventricular-fibrillation onset on research Holter series (SDDB). A rising τ_s in the approach means the RR–|ΔRR| ordinal graph reordered — not a bedside alarm and not a substitute for SDNN/RMSSD.
cardiology.example_dual:
How to write an honest result in this domain:

“In the pre-event approach, Δτ_s and Δexcess3 move in the same direction; the classical panel (variance↑, AR1↓) is ambiguous. Valid as a research-cohort / CCTP-method reading; not a medical device or bedside alert.”

Always state which variables, which cut (event), and what you are not claiming.
cardiology.example_recd:
RECD decomposes ordinal-pattern agreement (Bandt–Pompe symbols) between RR and |ΔRR| into layers Φ₁–Φ₃. excess3 summarizes synergy not explained by simpler layers and is often more robust when RR is noisy.

For teaching: read excess3 together with τ_s; do not treat a single binary Φ as “clinical risk”.
cardiology.jargon_note:
Minimal field glossary (to read papers and the Lab):
• RR — time between beats (ms)
• |ΔRR| — change from one beat to the next
• VF / vfon — ventricular fibrillation / onset mark on the series
• Holter, SDDB, CCTP — ambulatory recording, demo database, platform pilot protocol
• SDNN / RMSSD — classical variability (contrast panel; not τ_s)
• approach / pre-event — window just before the marker of interest
cardiology.methods_note: Lab preset «cardiology»: e.g. window W≈101, stride=5, θ₃≈0.08, proxy X=[z(RR), z(|ΔRR|)] aligned with the CCTP pilot. Document these parameters in any hand-in.
cardiology.maturity_phrase: v1.0 empirical maturity: highest on the platform (CCTP pilot / SDDB demos). Still: methodological proxy, not a clinical product.

epidemiology.variables: cases (incidence) and drivers (climate, humidity, vector index…)
epidemiology.event_name: outbreak onset / epidemic peak / surveillance change
epidemiology.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of ordinal cases–drivers coupling — not merely the incidence ramp.
epidemiology.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ irreducible synergy among epi channels when the transmission regime reorders.
epidemiology.classic_gloss: case var/AR1: rise with outbreaks and seasonality; not enough to claim coupling change alone.
epidemiology.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal incidence–drivers coupling across surveillance segments.
epidemiology.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): multi-channel ordinal synergy {trend} in the epidemic segment.
epidemiology.event_on: Cut at outbreak onset / peak t = {t}: pre-outbreak vs approach or post-peak contrast by design.
epidemiology.event_off: No outbreak date: half/half Δ (exploratory; better anchor to the epi week of interest).
epidemiology.summary_concord: Epi dual reading: cases–drivers relational reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Not an SEIR model or nowcast.
epidemiology.summary_quiet: Epi dual reading: little ordinal change; may be stable endemicity, surveillance noise, or W too long.
epidemiology.summary_mixed: Mixed epi dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Separate seasonality, reporting delays, and surveillance quality.
epidemiology.lab_coach: Guiding question: does the outbreak reorganize cases–climate coupling, or only scale incidence?
epidemiology.example_tau: In dengue-like demos, τ_s rises if cases and humidity stop co-varying as in endemicity even when mean cases only “go up”.
epidemiology.example_dual: Line: “Δτ_s and excess3 agree on the approach to the peak; interpret with seasonality and under-reporting — not an operational alert.”
epidemiology.example_recd: RECD on weekly series: short W (≈13); nested Φ read symbolic cases–climate coincidence, not R_t.
epidemiology.jargon_note: Jargon: incidence, outbreak, endemicity, epi week, surveillance, under-reporting, vector, seasonality, R_t (contrast).
epidemiology.methods_note: Epidemiology preset (short W, stride=1) for weekly/monthly surveillance series.
epidemiology.maturity_phrase: Maturity: mid-high pedagogical; synthetic demos + reading template, not an embedded real surveillance cohort.

neuroscience.variables: EEG / LFP channels (or band powers) multi-sensor
neuroscience.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ ordinal coupling across channels — symbolic synchrony grammar, not a spike detector or clinical SOZ tool.
neuroscience.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ multi-channel irreducible synergy when the ensemble reorganizes (toy pre-ictal or state switch).
neuroscience.classic_gloss: per-channel var/AR1: artifact- and filter-sensitive; classical panel does not replace montage QC.
neuroscience.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal EEG/LFP inter-channel coupling across segments.
neuroscience.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): multi-channel synergy {trend} at the state transition.
neuroscience.event_on: Cut at ictal onset / stimulus t = {t}: pre vs approach or peri-event Δ by protocol.
neuroscience.event_off: No state marker: half/half Δ (useful only if the series has a designed switch).
neuroscience.summary_concord: Neuro dual reading: inter-channel coupling reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Not an epilepsy diagnosis.
neuroscience.summary_quiet: Neuro dual reading: little ordinal change — stable state, artifacts, or W mismatched to sampling?
neuroscience.summary_mixed: Mixed neuro dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Prioritize preprocess, ICA/rejects, and channel parity.
neuroscience.lab_coach: Guiding question: did ensemble ordinal topology change, or only one noisy channel’s power?
neuroscience.example_tau: In a toy ictal demo, τ_s rises when two channels stop sharing the same Bandt–Pompe pattern in the approach.
neuroscience.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance pre-marker; artifacts reviewed; no clinical onset-zone claim.”
neuroscience.example_recd: Φ₁ reads symbol coincidence across sensors; excess3 captures higher-order ensemble synergy.
neuroscience.jargon_note: Jargon: EEG, LFP, ictal/pre-ictal, montage, artifact, ICA, band (δ/θ/α…), synchrony, SOZ (clinical contrast only).
neuroscience.methods_note: Neuroscience preset (W≈51): sensitive to sampling rate and length; document filters and rejects.
neuroscience.maturity_phrase: Maturity: mid pedagogical (toy coupling transition); not a clinical iEEG cohort.

ecology.event_name: bloom / threshold crossing / lake regime shift
ecology.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of nutrient–producer coupling (e.g. P–Chl), bridging lake CSD literature.
ecology.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ ordinal synergy when the lake reorganizes toward an alternate regime (toy eutrophication).
ecology.classic_gloss: var/AR1: classical lake CSD signatures; may diverge from the relational panel — read in parallel.
ecology.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal nutrient–biomass coupling across segments.
ecology.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): lake-ensemble ordinal synergy {trend}.
ecology.event_on: Cut at bloom / threshold t = {t}: pre-transition vs approach to the alternate regime.
ecology.event_off: No bloom date: half/half Δ (short series → interpret cautiously).
ecology.summary_concord: Ecology dual reading: nutrient–producer reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Not a eutrophication forecast.
ecology.summary_quiet: Ecology dual reading: little ordinal change; stable regime or series too short for W.
ecology.summary_mixed: Mixed ecology dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Cross var/AR1 and phenological seasonality.
ecology.lab_coach: Guiding question: does the lake reorganize P–Chl before the bloom, or only raise chlorophyll?
ecology.example_tau: In a eutrophication demo, τ_s changes when phosphorus and chlorophyll stop co-varying as in the oligotrophic state.
ecology.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance pre-bloom with partial classical CSD; LTER-like teaching demo, not reservoir management.”
ecology.example_recd: RECD with moderate W: sparse annual samples → do not demand Holter-like windows.
ecology.jargon_note: Jargon: eutrophication, bloom, chlorophyll-a, TP/P, alternate regime, CSD, resilience, LTER, oligo/meso/eutrophic.
ecology.methods_note: Ecology preset (W≈25): long but sparse series; prioritize sampling quality over huge n_surr.
ecology.maturity_phrase: Maturity: mid (classical CSD bridge); synthetic demos + guided reading.

finance.event_name: regime break / stress day / vol shift
finance.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of ordinal coupling among market series (not price prediction or alpha).
finance.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ irreducible synergy among factors/assets when the dependence regime reorders.
finance.classic_gloss: return var/AR1: rise in stress; not a trading signal and not the same as τ_s.
finance.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal multi-series market coupling across segments.
finance.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): multi-asset ordinal synergy {trend}.
finance.event_on: Cut at stress / break t = {t}: pre-regime vs volatility-approach contrast.
finance.event_off: No stress date: half/half Δ (exploratory; not a strategy backtest).
finance.summary_concord: Finance dual reading: dependence reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Illustrative — not investment advice.
finance.summary_quiet: Finance dual reading: stable ordinal regime in this segment; do not read as a “safe market”.
finance.summary_mixed: Mixed finance dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Separate microstructure noise from regime reorganization.
finance.lab_coach: Guiding question: did dependence topology change, or only vol amplitude?
finance.example_tau: In a regime demo, τ_s moves when returns and vol stop sharing the same ordinal pattern — without “predicting the crash”.
finance.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance on the approach to synthetic stress; teaching material, not a trading signal.”
finance.example_recd: RECD on standardized returns: short W (≈21); excess3 is not a Fama–French factor.
finance.jargon_note: Jargon: return, realized vol, regime, stress, crisis correlation, microstructure, alpha/beta (contrast only).
finance.methods_note: Finance preset (W≈21): pedagogical regimes; forbid portfolio performance claims.
finance.maturity_phrase: Maturity: low–mid pedagogical; transfer the method, do not trade markets.

climate.event_name: drought onset / flash drought / season shift
climate.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of temp–precip–soil coupling under latent drought — not a seasonal forecast.
climate.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ land–atmosphere ordinal synergy when the hydro-climate regime reorders.
climate.classic_gloss: var/AR1: useful classical regime EWS; may diverge from the multi-variable relational panel.
climate.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal temp–precip–soil coupling across segments.
climate.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): hydro-climate ordinal synergy {trend}.
climate.event_on: Cut at drought onset / event t = {t}: pre vs approach to hydro stress.
climate.event_off: No drought date: half/half Δ (regime demo; not a nowcast).
climate.summary_concord: Climate dual reading: hydro-climate reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Regime demo — not operational forecast.
climate.summary_quiet: Climate dual reading: little ordinal change; stable season or slow forcing outside W.
climate.summary_mixed: Mixed climate dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Consider seasonality and spatial scale of inputs.
climate.lab_coach: Guiding question: does hydro stress reorganize temp–precip–soil, or only shift means?
climate.example_tau: In drought-demo, τ_s changes when soil and precip stop co-varying as in the reference wet season.
climate.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance pre-synthetic drought; classroom material, not a weather warning.”
climate.example_recd: Multi-variable climate RECD: excess3 does not replace SPI/SPEI; different question.
climate.jargon_note: Jargon: drought, flash drought, soil moisture, precip, anomaly, regime, teleconnection (contrast), SPI/SPEI.
climate.methods_note: Climate preset (W≈21): synthetic regime series; document temporal aggregation of inputs.
climate.maturity_phrase: Maturity: mid pedagogical (regime); no climate prediction engine.

social.variables: mean opinion, polarization, message exposure (toy proxy)
social.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of coupling among collective indicators — social-systems grammar, not electoral prediction.
social.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ synergy among opinion channels when the polarization regime reorders.
social.classic_gloss: opinion-index var/AR1: rise with social volatility; do not prove “causal virality”.
social.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal coupling among collective indicators across segments.
social.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): social-ensemble ordinal synergy {trend}.
social.event_on: Cut at switch / shock t = {t}: pre vs approach to the polarized regime.
social.event_off: No marked switch: half/half Δ (prefer demos with designed ground truth).
social.summary_concord: Social dual reading: collective reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Toy model — not social prediction.
social.summary_quiet: Social dual reading: stable ordinal regime; not empirical “social peace”.
social.summary_mixed: Mixed social dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Remember: the demo switch is designed.
social.lab_coach: Guiding question: does polarization reorganize cross-channel coupling, or only move one index mean?
social.example_tau: In polarization-demo, τ_s jumps when opinion and exposure leave the moderate-regime ordinal pattern.
social.example_dual: Line: “Concordance on the approach to the synthetic switch; teaches grammar, not policy.”
social.example_recd: RECD on collective series: excess3 is not a validated “radicalization index”.
social.jargon_note: Jargon: polarization, echo chamber (metaphor), information shock, aggregate opinion, collective regime.
social.methods_note: Social preset: designed-switch demo; always declare synthetic character.
social.maturity_phrase: Maturity: low–mid pedagogical (toy model with ground truth).

education.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of coupling among collective learning signals — sandbox, not production LMS analytics.
education.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ synergy between participation and performance when the cohort regime reorders.
education.classic_gloss: grade/click var/AR1: noisy and biased; do not prove “pedagogical effectiveness” alone.
education.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal coupling among cohort signals across segments.
education.event_on: Cut at intervention / unit change t = {t}: pre vs post pedagogical design contrast.
education.event_off: No unit marker: half/half Δ (useful in fatigue-switch demos).
education.summary_concord: Learning dual reading: cohort reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Sandbox — not evaluation of real students.
education.summary_quiet: Learning dual reading: little ordinal change; stable cohort or uninformative metrics.
education.summary_mixed: Mixed learning dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Do not use this to grade people.
education.lab_coach: Guiding question: does the intervention reorganize participation–performance, or only shift averages?
education.example_tau: In education-cohort-demo, τ_s moves when engagement and performance leave the previous unit’s regime.
education.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance after synthetic switch; classroom tool for Tau, not an LMS dashboard.”
education.example_recd: RECD on a synthetic cohort: teach Φ/excess3 without sensitive personal data.
education.jargon_note: Jargon: cohort, engagement, instructional unit, intervention, fatigue, LMS (production contrast).
education.methods_note: Education preset (W≈17): short cohort series; ethics: synthetic or consented data only.

physiology.variables: sleep / HRV / fragmentation proxy (toy or bridge series)
physiology.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ reorganization of coupling among sleep/HRV physiological signals — not clinical AASM scoring.
physiology.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ ordinal synergy when (proxy) sleep architecture reorders.
physiology.classic_gloss: HRV var/AR1: classical but noisy; do not replace a validated hypnogram.
physiology.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in ordinal sleep/HRV multi-signal coupling across segments.
physiology.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): physiological ordinal synergy {trend}.
physiology.event_on: Cut at fragmentation / transition t = {t}: pre vs approach to the altered state.
physiology.event_off: No stage marker: half/half Δ (physiological-bridge demo).
physiology.summary_concord: Sleep/HRV dual reading: physiological reorganization (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Pedagogical bridge — not a sleep diagnosis.
physiology.summary_quiet: Physiology dual reading: little ordinal change; check signal quality and proxy-night length.
physiology.summary_mixed: Mixed physiology dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Do not claim NREM/REM stages without scoring.
physiology.lab_coach: Guiding question: does fragmentation reorganize multi-signal coupling, or only raise one channel’s var?
physiology.example_tau: In sleep-fragmentation-demo, τ_s tracks the designed fragmentation-regime switch.
physiology.example_dual: Line: “Relational concordance on a fragmentation demo; not a polysomnography report.”
physiology.example_recd: RECD as cardio–sleep bridge: same grammar as CCTP, different empirical pretension.
physiology.jargon_note: Jargon: HRV, fragmentation, hypnogram, NREM/REM (contrast), arousal, polysomnography, AASM (no scoring here).
physiology.methods_note: Physiology preset (W≈51): bridge to cardio; declare clinical limits.

synthetic.variables: coupled synthetic channels (e.g. coupled logistic x0, x1)
synthetic.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ ordinal coupling across channels with known ground truth — dual-reading calibration bench.
synthetic.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ ordinal synergy after the designed coupling switch; compare to the planted event.
synthetic.classic_gloss: var/AR1: sometimes confirm, sometimes silent; use the switch to see controlled discordance.
synthetic.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} in synthetic ordinal coupling across segments (ground truth available).
synthetic.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): ordinal synergy {trend} relative to the designed switch.
synthetic.event_on: Coupling switch at t = {t}: pre vs post contrast with design truth.
synthetic.event_off: No marked switch: half/half Δ (still useful, but you lose explicit ground truth).
synthetic.summary_concord: Synthetic dual reading: ordinal reorganization aligned with design (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}. Ideal to calibrate the eye.
synthetic.summary_quiet: Synthetic dual reading: small Δ — lower noise, raise post-switch coupling, or revisit W.
synthetic.summary_mixed: Mixed synthetic dual reading (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Explore W/stride and n_surr before real data.
synthetic.lab_coach: Guiding question: with ground truth, does your dual reading recover the switch without overclaim?
synthetic.example_tau: In coupled-logistic, τ_s should react to the designed post-switch coupling increase.
synthetic.example_dual: Line: “Post-switch synthetic concordance with qualified p_surr; ready to transfer the grammar to a real domain.”
synthetic.example_recd: RECD on synthetic data: check that excess3 is not merely an echo of univariate var.
synthetic.jargon_note: Jargon: ground truth, switch, coupling, surrogate, phase-shuffle, calibration, positive control.
synthetic.methods_note: Synthetic preset: demos with planted event; cite seed and generator in the report.
synthetic.maturity_phrase: Maturity: pedagogical control (design truth), not field empirical evidence.

generic.event_name: event / temporal cut of the design
generic.tau_gloss: τ_s ≈ mean pairwise ordinal coupling in windows — relational reorganization, not univariate magnitude.
generic.excess_gloss: excess3 ≈ irreducible ordinal synergy (continuous Level 3) across channels.
generic.classic_gloss: var/AR1: classical univariate EWS panel; may confirm, stay silent, or contradict τ_s/excess3.
generic.bullet_dtau: Δτ_s = {d_tau:+.4f} ({mag}): {direction} of ordinal coupling across segments.
generic.bullet_excess: mean excess3 = {mean_ex:.4f}; Δexcess3 = {d_ex:+.4f} ({mag}): irreducible ordinal synergy {trend} in the segment.
generic.event_on: Event marked at t = {t}: pre/post Δ use that cut.
generic.event_off: No event index: Δ uses first vs second half split (exploratory).
generic.summary_concord: Dual reading: τ_s and excess3 move in the same direction (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}){p_part}.
generic.summary_quiet: Dual reading: weak ordinal reorganization in this design (small Δ).
generic.summary_mixed: Mixed dual reading: τ_s and excess3 disagree (Δτ_s={d_tau:+.3f}, Δexcess3={d_ex:+.3f}). Prioritize context and classical EWS.
generic.lab_coach: Qualify the conclusion by domain empirical maturity and cut design.
generic.example_tau: Pick a domain (cardio, epi, …) for jargon examples; τ_s math is the same.
generic.example_dual: Template: report Δτ_s, Δexcess3, p_surr, and a scope sentence (what you do not claim).
generic.example_recd: Φ₁–Φ₃ + excess3: nested grammar; excess3 primary under noise.
generic.jargon_note: Use the domain sheet for field-specific jargon.
generic.methods_note: Generic or synthetic preset depending on the case.
generic.maturity_phrase: Maturity: qualify by the chosen domain.

domain_voice_ui.panel_header: How Tau is talked about in this domain
domain_voice_ui.event: Event or cut of interest (what you compare before/after)
domain_voice_ui.coach: Guiding question for reading the result
domain_voice_ui.jargon: Field vocabulary (so it is not just software jargon)
domain_voice_ui.lens_help: Pick a scientific or professional field. You will see what τ_s and RECD mean in that vocabulary. The formula stays the same; the interpretation changes.
domain_voice_ui.example_tau_h: What τ_s measures here (in plain language)
domain_voice_ui.example_dual_h: How to read a result in this field
domain_voice_ui.example_recd_h: What RECD and excess3 add in this context
domain_voice_ui.lab_panel: How to interpret Tau in the chosen domain
domain_voice_ui.why_here: This box translates the module’s abstract idea into an example from that field. It is not a medical diagnosis, not an automatic forecast, and not an operational alert.
domain_voice_ui.math_unchanged: The math of τ_s / RECD is the same across domains; what changes are the variables, the event of interest, and what you are allowed to claim.
domain_voice_ui.missing_body: No written example for «{domain}» in this language. Pick another domain or reload the page after updating the materials.

auth.guest_hint: Sign in to save progress and exams.
auth.register_hint: Local account on this server (JSON + PBKDF2). Min. 6 characters. Not a cloud SaaS.
auth.welcome_back: Welcome back, {name}. Your progress is stored in this environment.
auth.err_password_short: Password must be at least 6 characters.
auth.err_username_taken: Username already taken. Choose another or sign in.
auth.topbar_hint: Local account on this server. Also available in the sidebar.
auth.session_persistent: Active session in this app. Progress is kept when you return to the same environment.

eval.title: Assessments and progress
eval.subtitle: Per-module auto-graded exams, weighted course score, and activity log — with a local account.
eval.goal_1: Create an account or sign in to resume your work.
eval.goal_2: Complete each weekly quiz (W1–W6) and see the automatic score.
eval.goal_3: Track formative/summative tasks and the activity log.
eval.account_callout: Credentials and progress are stored only in this environment (data/student_records/). Suitable for classroom or local laptop. Do not reuse passwords from other services.
eval.login_required: Sign in or create an account to view exams, course grade, and task history.
eval.progress_missing: Could not load progress. Sign out and sign in again.
eval.grade_empty: No exam attempts yet. Choose a module below.
eval.weights_blurb: Weights match syllabus §6.1: formatives W1–W3 **15%** (5% each) · cardio report W4 **30%** · transfer W5 **25%** · portfolio W6 **30%**. Uses the **best** score per module.
eval.modules_callout: Auto-graded multiple choice. Quiz pass threshold: **{thr}%**. After submit you get per-question feedback. Retakes allowed; best score is kept.
eval.module_missing: Module not found in the question bank.
eval.result_fail: Below threshold: {score}/{max} ({pct}%). Threshold {thr}%. Review explanations and retry.
eval.tasks_blurb: Mark syllabus deliverables (essays and reports). They are not auto-graded here; they complement the numeric quizzes.
eval.task_w2: W2 · Δτ_s table + hash + near-null control

curriculum.quiz_note: The auto-graded quiz complements (does not replace) the 0–2 Lab report rubric.
curriculum.w1_g1: Explain var and AR(1) as univariate EWS.
curriculum.w1_g2: State at least two conceptual failures of the classical panel.
curriculum.w1_g3: Cite the ethical scope of the software (not a device).
curriculum.w1_lab: Lab: synthetic / EWS-only (coupled logistic or AR noise)
curriculum.w1_deliv: Short essay (~1 p.): “When does a variance rise not answer the system question?”
curriculum.w2_g2: Interpret windowed τ_s and record repro_hash.
curriculum.w2_g3: Compare synthetic switch vs AR/noise control.
curriculum.w2_deliv: Table: Δτ_s, p_surr, hash; 5 lines without overclaim.
curriculum.w3_title: Nested RECD, excess3 and dual reading
curriculum.w3_g1: Describe Φ₁–Φ₃ and the role of excess3 under noise.
curriculum.w3_g3: Do not base the claim only on TDA/breathing extensions.
curriculum.w3_lab: Lab: RECD panel + dual summary · Fast
curriculum.w3_deliv: Mini-report 1–2 pp. with question, params, dual, scope.
curriculum.w4_g2: Write dual reading with domain voice and disclaimers.
curriculum.w4_lab: Lab: sddb_rr_38_demo or cardio-like · cardiology preset
curriculum.w4_deliv: Dual report 2–3 pp. (30% portfolio): metrics, p_surr, allowed/forbidden phrases, hash.
curriculum.w5_g1: Reuse τ_s/RECD grammar in one transfer domain.
curriculum.w5_g3: Justify preset and do not export cardio-anchor claims.
curriculum.w5_lab: Lab: e.g. dengue_like_demo (or another transfer domain)
curriculum.w5_deliv: Transfer mini-report 1–2 pp. (25%): event analogue, proxy, what is not claimed.
curriculum.w6_g2: Use extensions as contrast, not as core.
curriculum.w6_lab: Lab: optional Full re-analysis + MD/JSON/Methods export
curriculum.w6_deliv: Final portfolio (45% of sumative S4–S6 block): reports + exports + checklist + ½ p. ethics.

rubric.interactive_callout: Mark 0, 1 or 2 per criterion (max 12). Practice pass threshold: ≥ 9. Excellent: ≥ 11. Export Markdown for the LMS.
rubric.export_footer: STP syllabus §6.2 · 6 criteria × 0–2 = 12 points · generated in Teaching
rubric.c3_1: Reports Δ but no p_surr or context
rubric.c3_2: Δτ_s, mean/Δ excess3, p_surr (or declared n_surr=0)
rubric.c5_2: Claims aligned to maturity + non-scope sentence
